prerequisites: A running MySQL server reachable at the host and port you will configure (host, port).
The name of the target database to read from (database).
A MySQL user account with permission to read the target tables, supplied via username and password.
A replication method selected for syncs (replication_method): a standard query-based read or binary-log CDC. CDC requires the binary log to be enabled and a user with replication privileges.
If connecting over TLS, the CA certificate (and optionally a client certificate, client key, and key password) for the chosen ssl_mode.
If the database is not directly reachable, an SSH bastion host plus an SSH key or tunnel user password for tunnel_method.

authMethods.name: Username and password
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a MySQL user account using the username and password config fields. MySQL validates credentials with its pluggable authentication system; MySQL 8.0 and later use caching_sha2_password as the default plugin (mysql_native_password is also available). Grant the account read access to the target tables, and replication privileges if you select binary-log CDC.

authMethods.name: TLS/SSL encrypted connection
authMethods.summary: Encrypt the connection by setting ssl_mode. Supply the server CA certificate via ssl_mode.ca_certificate to verify the server, and optionally provide ssl_mode.client_certificate, ssl_mode.client_key, and ssl_mode.client_key_password for mutual TLS client-certificate authentication. This secures the transport on top of username/password authentication.

authMethods.name: SSH tunnel
authMethods.summary: Reach a MySQL server that is not directly exposed by routing the connection through an SSH bastion using tunnel_method. Authenticate the SSH session with either an SSH private key (tunnel_method.ssh_key) or an SSH user password (tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password). This is a network transport option layered on top of the MySQL credentials, not a MySQL login method.

setupSteps.title: Locate the MySQL server connection details
setupSteps.body: Determine the hostname or IP and TCP port of the MySQL server (default port 3306) and the name of the database to read. Enter these as host, port, and database. Ensure the server is reachable from where the connector runs; if not, plan to use the SSH tunnel option.

setupSteps.title: Create a dedicated MySQL user and grant read access
setupSteps.body: Use CREATE USER to add an account, then GRANT it SELECT on the target database (for example, GRANT SELECT ON your_db.* TO 'user'@'%'). Supply this account as username and password. Per the MySQL access-control model, grant only the privileges needed to read the tables you intend to sync.

setupSteps.title: Choose a replication method
setupSteps.body: Set replication_method. Use the standard query-based method for a straightforward read of tables. To capture inserts, updates, and deletes via the binary log (CDC), enable binary logging on the server and grant the user replication privileges (such as REPLICATION SLAVE and REPLICATION CLIENT) using GRANT.

setupSteps.title: Configure TLS encryption (optional)
setupSteps.body: To encrypt traffic, set ssl_mode and provide the server CA certificate in ssl_mode.ca_certificate. For client-certificate (mutual TLS) authentication, also configure the server with ssl-ca, ssl-cert, and ssl-key and supply ssl_mode.client_certificate, ssl_mode.client_key, and, if the key is encrypted, ssl_mode.client_key_password.

setupSteps.title: Configure an SSH tunnel (optional)
setupSteps.body: If the server is only reachable through a bastion, set tunnel_method and provide the SSH host and tunnel user. Authenticate with either tunnel_method.ssh_key (private key) or tunnel_method.tunnel_user_password.

setupSteps.title: Set optional tuning fields and test the connection
setupSteps.body: Optionally adjust check_privileges, jdbc_url_params, max_db_connections, concurrency, checkpoint_target_interval_seconds, table_filters, and treat_tinyint1_as_integer as needed. Save and run a connection check to confirm the credentials and network path work.
